phase-scope.phase-38: Plan DE-hosted MTProto runtime and RU public TCP 443 SNI router to replace the blocked RU-only Phase-37 live edge.

commands.command-4: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_domain_tls_edge_static.py -v
commands.command-5: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/modules/M-050.xml docs/plans/Phase-38.xml docs/verification/V-M-050.xml && mygrace lint
commands.command-6: operator-live: RU SNI router owns public 443, RU nginx fallback works, DE runtime listens privately/publicly as designed, private policy API rejects public access, active SNI replay succeeds, and Telegram Desktop marks the proxy available.

verification-levels.module-local: Static config smoke, syntax checks, backend bridge tests with fake/private policy targets, XML parse, and MyGRACE lint.
verification-levels.wave-level: M-044, M-046, M-048, M-049, and M-050 must pass together because routing, deployment, policy apply, and runtime ownership form one edge behavior.
verification-levels.phase-level: Operator-live proof on RU and DE validates HTTPS, private policy API, assignment replay, DE Telegram upstream, and Telegram Desktop availability.

module-checks.check-1: RU SNI router config has mutually exclusive web and MTProto routes with an explicit unknown-SNI behavior.
module-checks.check-2: DE runtime env contains no blank MTProto secret, salt, token, cert, or policy settings.
module-checks.check-3: Backend bridge can target a private DE policy API URL and redacts token and SNI secret material.
module-checks.check-4: Existing assignments replay to DE runtime without reissue or secret rotation.
module-checks.check-5: Static smoke rejects configs that expose DE policy API on 0.0.0.0 without firewall/private binding.
module-checks.check-6: RU public HTTPS remains healthy through the SNI router.
module-checks.check-7: DE runtime reaches Telegram upstream and Telegram Desktop marks the issued proxy available.

scenarios.scenario-1: Browser request for krotpn.xyz routes from RU public 443 to RU nginx 8443 and returns a healthy HTTPS page.
scenarios.scenario-2: Telegram request for an issued user SNI routes from RU public 443 to DE MTProto runtime and becomes available in Telegram Desktop.
scenarios.scenario-3: Backend startup replay loads active assignment policies into DE runtime after restart.
scenarios.scenario-4: DE policy API unavailable returns degraded MTProto state and does not claim an active usable proxy.
scenarios.scenario-5: Public access to DE policy API is rejected by bind address, firewall, token auth, or all three.
scenarios.scenario-6: SNI router failure rolls back to nginx-only public 443 before user traffic is advertised.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Never print MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN, MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX, MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT, derived fake-TLS secrets, full tg links, private keys, JWTs, refresh tokens, Resend API keys, or email verification tokens.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Live evidence may include role names, port ownership, status codes, policy counts, and redacted SNI suffixes only.

live-smoke-gates.gate-1: RU ss output shows public TCP 443 owned by the SNI router and nginx HTTPS fallback on 8443.
live-smoke-gates.gate-2: DE ss output shows MTProto runtime listening on its configured target port.
live-smoke-gates.gate-3: RU backend policy health succeeds to DE over the private path with the runtime token.
live-smoke-gates.gate-4: Public internet cannot access the DE policy API.
live-smoke-gates.gate-5: curl https://krotpn.xyz returns a healthy frontend response without TLS bypass.
live-smoke-gates.gate-6: Existing active assignment SNI replay returns success and policy count is non-zero.
live-smoke-gates.gate-7: Operator Telegram Desktop retest marks the proxy available.

autonomous-execution-safety.safe-local: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, static smoke, syntax checks, compose config, and backend tests with fake/private policy targets.
autonomous-execution-safety.requires-operator: Public routing changes, DE runtime rollout, wildcard certificate/private key placement, and Telegram Desktop proof.
